panels.title: GC p95 Duration
panels.description: Shows the 95th-percentile garbage collection pause duration. Elevated values indicate GC pressure that may cause application latency spikes.

panels.title: GC p99 Duration
panels.description: Shows the 99th-percentile garbage collection pause duration. Tail-latency GC pauses here directly impact worst-case response times.

panels.title: JVM CPU Time
panels.description: Shows the total CPU time consumed by the JVM process. Compare against host CPU capacity to gauge JVM overhead.

panels.title: JVM CPU Utilization
panels.description: Shows the recent CPU utilization of the JVM process as a percentage. Sustained high values may require scaling or code optimization.

panels.title: Loaded Classes
panels.description: Shows the number of classes currently loaded in the JVM. A continuously increasing count may indicate a classloader leak.

panels.title: JVM Memory (Used / Committed / Limit)
panels.description: Tracks heap and non-heap memory pools showing used, committed, and max limits. Watch for used memory approaching the limit, which triggers aggressive GC.

panels.title: GC Collections by Collector
panels.description: Shows garbage collection invocation counts grouped by collector type. Frequent full GC cycles suggest heap sizing or memory leak issues.

panels.title: Thread Count by Daemon Status
panels.description: Tracks JVM thread counts grouped by daemon versus non-daemon threads. A sudden increase can indicate thread leaks or runaway background work.

panels.title: Buffer Pool Memory Usage
panels.description: Shows direct and mapped buffer pool memory consumption. Rising buffer usage without release may indicate NIO resource leaks.
